sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT, info STRING);
SELECT id, regexp_extract(info, '([a-z]+)_([0-9]+)', 1) AS extracted_text, (2 ** 3) AS power FROM test_table WHERE info IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, (regexp_extract(info, '([a-z]+)_([0-9]+)', 2) ** 2) AS squared_value FROM test_table WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE info IS NOT NULL) HAVING squared_value > 100;
SELECT id, power, regexp_extract(info, '([a-z]+)_?', 0) AS full_match FROM (SELECT id, (2 ** id) AS power FROM test_table) t WHERE info IS NOT NULL AND power < 20;
CREATE TABLE another_table (val FLOAT, description STRING);

SELECT t.id, a.val ** 2 AS squared_value, regexp_extract(a.description, '([a-z]+)_([0-9]+)', 2) AS extracted_number FROM test_table t JOIN another_table a ON t.id = CAST(a.val AS INT) WHERE a.val IS NOT NULL;

WITH RECURSIVE numbers AS (SELECT 1 AS num UNION ALL SELECT num + 1 FROM numbers WHERE num < 10) SELECT regexp_extract('test_number_', '([a-z]+)_(\d+)', 1) AS group1, (num ** 2) AS squared_num FROM numbers;
CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE temp_table AS SELECT id, regexp_extract(info, '^(foo|bar)_([0-9]+)$', 2) AS category FROM test_table WHERE info IS NOT NULL;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM temp_table GROUP BY category HAVING COUNT(*) > 0;
